educational_objective: Name faces and early feelings with short shared books at 19-21m.
age_stage_nuance: Big feelings arrive faster than words; faces and colour maps help more than talk.
what_to_look_for: Board or short share aloud, concrete faces/moods, ISBN-verified UK primary.
what_to_avoid: Waterstones 403 primaries, wrong ISBN soft pages, KS1 therapy workbooks.
methodology: Benchmarked UK book retailers (Wordery, LoveReading4Kids) with ISBN-verified titles for 19-21m. Captured structured age_signals from each live primary. Ranked with buying_factor_memo for stage fit and review depth. Every Top Pick primary was URL-verified (HTTP smoke) then availability-checked buyable before inbox lock.
buying_factor_memo: Face spotting first, then colour maps, then body feel language, then selective share alouds. Ownership multiple covers stockist depth and format mix. Max two Libby Walden.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for colour linked big feelings
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A board book Colour Monster that maps muddled feelings to colours, so you can pause on one jar or colour instead of turning it into a long talk.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Big feelings at nearly two rarely need a worksheet. One colour at a time keeps the sit short and concrete on a tired evening. Colour linked big feelings help when they already know colours and need a handle for cross or quiet without a long talk.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1: clearest early feelings map with huge UK depth.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A small Usborne faces board book for spotting eyes, mouths and expressions before longer feelings stories take over. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Before mood stories land, many toddlers still need plain faces. Keep it to a few pages. Plain face spotting helps when expression books feel too wordy. Read two pages on the sofa, name the face, then close the book while it still feels friendly.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for body and face flap play
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lift the flap classic hiding belly buttons, toes and faces so body and face words become a search game on your lap.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Flaps make face and body noticing a game, not a quiz. Useful when feelings talk starts with “where’s your tummy?” Body and face flap play helps when they point at themselves first. One short go with Where Is Baby's Belly is enough before you tidy away.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A monster faces book that pairs simple mood words with bold pictures you can copy with your own face for a short sit.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Copying a glad or sad face is more useful than naming a chart. Keep the joke gentle. Monster mood masks help when they already mimic faces in the mirror. Stay nearby while they try Glad Monster, Sad Monster, then stop on a good note.

top_picks.product_name: In My Heart: A Book of Feelings
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A die cut heart book that describes anger, quiet and bravery in the body, shared a page or two at a time on the sofa.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Some toddlers need body language for feelings before story plots. Stop early if the page gets heavy. Body feeling words help when cross shows up as a stiff body more than a word at tea. Put In My Heart: A where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for peep through emotion pages
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A peep through feelings board book pairing simple emotion words with warm pictures for short naming sits. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Peep holes slow the turn and keep hands busy while you name the mood. Stay concrete. peep through emotion pages help when they swipe story books too fast. Put Feelings where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for picking one feeling page
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A feelings catalogue you can open on one page that matches today, rather than reading cover to cover with a restless toddler.
top_picks.ember_verdict: You do not need the whole book. Pick the page that matches the morning. Picking one feeling page helps when big feelings arrive mid chaos and you need a quick shared picture. Let them finish the turn with How Are You Feeling, then close the activity kindly.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A rhyming mood swing picture book for short aloud sits when silly, angry and quiet days need naming without a talk.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Mood swings are the plot at this age. Keep the rhyme playful and stop when they wriggle. Mood swing stories help when the day has already done three feelings before lunch. If interest fades, pack Today I Feel Silly away and try again another day.

top_picks.product_name: Feelings: Inside my heart and in my head...
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A peep through paperback of feelings verse with warm pictures, better as a borrowed quiet sit than a first board buy.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When board books feel done, this adds gentler language without becoming a therapy handout. Calmer feelings verse helps when you want one quieter option on the shelf. Use Feelings: Inside my heart as the second option when the first favourite is already out.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for alternate Colour Monster stockist
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: The same Colour Monster board book via LoveReading4Kids when you want a second UK buy path besides Wordery. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Ownership depth means a second stockist for the same trusted title, not a second talk. Alternate Colour Monster stockists help when Wordery is slow and you still want this map. Keep the session short with The Colour Monster so it stays a friendly invite.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #10: ownership multiple stockist depth for #1 title.

longlist.rank_rationale: #1: clearest early feelings map with huge UK depth.

ISBN keys the page; titles re-verified via og:title.
Fails Well Duh / Human parent for 19-21m.

qa_summary.notes: Mode A research 2026-07-24. URLs pending FF smoke+availability. Copy ship-checklist run at source.
